---
listing:
  - id: cognitive-pubs
    contents: "/publications/*/*/index.qmd"
    sort:
      - "pub-status asc"
      - "date desc"
    template: /assets/listings/pub-listing-scroll.ejs.md
    fields: [title, pub-authors, pub-year, venue, pub-status, pub-type, categories, pdf, data, code, doi, citation-count, altmetric-count]
    filter-ui: false
    include:
      projects: cognitive


  - id: brain-pubs
    contents: "/publications/*/*/index.qmd"
    sort:
      - "pub-status asc"
      - "date desc"
    template: /assets/listings/pub-listing-scroll.ejs.md
    fields: [title, pub-authors, pub-year, venue, pub-status, pub-type, categories, pdf, data, code, doi, citation-count, altmetric-count]
    filter-ui: false
    include:
      projects: brain

  - id: mindfulness-pubs
    contents: "/publications/*/*/index.qmd"
    sort:
      - "pub-status asc"
      - "date desc"
    template: /assets/listings/pub-listing-scroll.ejs.md
    fields: [title, pub-authors, pub-year, venue, pub-status, pub-type, categories, pdf, data, code, doi, citation-count, altmetric-count]
    filter-ui: false
    include:
      projects: mindfulness



page-layout: full
---

::: {.page-header}

::: {.header-text}
::: {.header-title}
PROJECTS
:::

::: {.header-subtitle}
Research areas and ongoing lines of work in the CALM Lab.
:::
:::

:::

::: hero-intro
The CALM Lab’s research is organized around three interconnected areas that together aim to understand attention across contexts of life and mind.
:::

:::::::::::: {.grid-3 .section-shell}
::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-blue}
<a href="#cognitive-section">

### Cognitive Mechanisms

How do differences in the ability to control attention relate to cognition and well-being?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>
:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-yellow}
<a href="#brain-section">

### Neural Mechanisms

What are the neural mechanisms underlying the ability to control attention?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::: {.card .card-interactive .quick-card .is-green}
<a href="#mindfulness-section">

### Mindfulness

How can mindfulness and contemplative practices strengthen attention control and well-being?

[View Project →]{.link-cta}</a>

:::::

::::::::::::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Individual Differences and Cognitive Mechanisms {#cognitive-section}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 700;"}
How do differences in the ability to control attention relate to cognition and well-being?
:::

::: {.profile-section-card}
People differ in their ability to control attention, and these differences are a fundamental aspect of human cognition. We investigate how individual differences in attention control play a role in shaping our perception, memory, reasoning, and well-being.

Critical to understanding the role of attention control in cognition and well-being is the ability to measure it. We investigate how to best measure attention control, including the development of new tasks and approaches to measuring attention control in real-world contexts.

[View and Download Attention Tasks →](){.link-cta}

:::

### Publications

<div class="pub-filter-bar" data-filter-by="categories" data-target="#listing-cognitive-pubs .listing-scroll-row">
  <button class="pill pill-filter active" data-filter="all">All</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="measurement">Measurement</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="sustained-attention">Sustained Attention</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="sensation">Sensation & Perception</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="cognition">Higher-Order Cognition</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="real-world">Real-World</button>
</div>

::: {#cognitive-pubs}
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Neural Mechanisms {#brain-section}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 700;"}
What are the neural mechanisms underlying the ability to control attention?
:::

::: {.profile-section-card}

We investigate the neural mechanisms underlying attention control, including the role of large-scale brain networks and their interactions in supporting attention control across contexts of life and mind.

:::

### Publications

<div class="pub-filter-bar" data-filter-by="categories" data-target="#listing-brain-pubs .listing-scroll-row">
  <button class="pill pill-filter active" data-filter="all">All</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="locus-coeruleus">Locus Coeruleus</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="brain-networks">Brain Networks</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="eeg">EEG</button>
  <button class="pill pill-filter" data-filter="sustained-attention">Sustained Attention</button>
</div>

::: {#brain-pubs}
:::

------------------------------------------------------------------------

# Mindfulness and Contemplative Practices {#mindfulness-section}

::: {style="text-align: center; color: var(--color-secondary); font-size: 1.3rem; font-weight: 700;"}
How can mindfulness and contemplative practices strengthen attention control and well-being?
:::

::: {.profile-section-card}

Mindfulness and related contemplative practices have been shown to influence attention, cognitive performance, and resilience. We investigate how these practices can strengthen attention control and promote well-being.

:::

### Publications

<div class="pub-filter-bar" data-filter-by="categories" data-target="#listing-mindfulness-pubs .listing-scroll-row">
  <button class="pill pill-filter active" data-filter="all">All</button>
</div>

::: {#mindfulness-pubs}
:::
